ogTitle: Great Mackerel Beach vs Green Cape: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Great Mackerel Beach and Green Cape.
heading: Great Mackerel Beach vs Green Cape — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Great Mackerel Beach, New South Wales and Green Cape, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Green Cape has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Great Mackerel Beach (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Great Mackerel Beach or Green Cape safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Great Mackerel Beach has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Green Cape scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Great Mackerel Beach scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Green Cape scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Great Mackerel Beach or Green Cape?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Green Cape has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
